explicit_text: My family moved to Tulsa when my father was transferred there. I loved going to the Tulsa Train Station to pick up my grandmother when she visited from Houston. The art deco architecture of the station fascinated me from a young age, and I still attend concerts there. Tulsa was also where I explored my interest in architecture and music, visiting churches downtown to play their pipe organs.
implicit_text: My family moved to a city in northeastern Oklahoma when my father was transferred there. The art deco architecture of the station fascinated me from a young age, and I still attend concerts there. That city was also where I explored my interest in architecture and music, visiting churches downtown to play their pipe organs.

explicit_text: My grandmother would take the train from Houston to Tulsa to visit us a couple of times a year. I remember those trips fondly because I enjoyed picking her up at the Tulsa Train Station. Houston was the place she lived, and it connected our family across states during my childhood.

explicit_text: I spent a lot of time at the Philbrook Museum of Art as a child, especially fascinated by Clark Field, who gave talks in the basement about his basket collections. Over the years, I worked on many projects at Philbrook and eventually became the event planner there. The museum was a significant part of my life both personally and professionally.

explicit_text: In the late 1950s, before we had a TV at home, we used to watch I Love Lucy every Wednesday night at our next-door neighbor's house. It was one of the first television programs I remember enjoying, and it marked the beginning of my family's embrace of modern culture when we finally got our own TV.

explicit_text: I attended Oklahoma State University where I had some wonderful professors, including Christine Salmon, a doctor of architecture. She took us on field trips to New York and introduced us to incredible ideas and people. OSU was where I developed a strong foundation in interior design and architecture, learning philosophies like 'Less is more' from mid-century architects.

explicit_text: One of the architectural philosophies I learned about was from Mies van der Rohe, famous for the quote 'Less is more.' explicit_text: The University of Tulsa came to own the McBirney Mansion twice, once after it was sold by a couple who renovated it and later again after several owners. explicit_text: John was diagnosed with ALS in August 2011 after noticing progressive weakness and difficulty running. ALS is a devastating disease that affected his muscles, breathing, and swallowing. I witnessed the rapid progression and the challenges it brought, including the need for medical interventions and eventual loss of function.
